data.content: The project uses **MSTest 3.8.0** with the Microsoft Testing Platform enabled (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`). Tests are categorized via `[TestCategory]`. I'll run only the Integration tests:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run integration tests with dotnet test

data.reasoningText:
The project uses **MSTest** (v3.8.0) with the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is set to `true` in `Directory.Build.props`). The tests are categorized as "Unit" and "Integration". The user wants to run only the integration tests.

With MSTest and the Microsoft Testing Platform, to filter by category I can use `--filter "TestCategory=Integration"`.

arguments.description: Run integration tests with dotnet test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-40a72378389a4f22b68c68e3c3f490ad && dotnet test --filter \"TestCategory=Integration\" 2>&1", "description": "Run integration tests with dotnet test", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content:
All done! The project uses **MSTest** with the **Microsoft Testing Platform**. The integration test (`GetAll_QueriesDatabase`) passed successfully using:

```
dotnet test --filter "TestCategory=Integration"
```
